core_thesis: The Mother's central claim is that Integral Yoga must extend realization into the material and cellular field: consciousness is not fulfilled by inward liberation alone but presses toward a supramental transformation of nature. Hence surrender, aspiration, and plasticity are not merely psychological disciplines; they are operations by which the Divine Śakti reorganizes mind, life, and body for a new mode of terrestrial being ([Mother's Agenda I, 1951](cite://the-mother/mothers-agenda/1.1951); [Questions and Answers 1954, Jan 6](cite://the-mother/questions-and-answers-1954/1954-01-06)).
